commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 29. "Take Thou from me the way of iniquity" (ver. 29). And since the law of works hath entered in, that sin might abound; he addeth, "And pity me according to Thy law." By what law, save by the law of faith? Hear the Apostle: "Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? Of works. Nay: but by the law of faith." This is the law of faith, whereby we believe and pray that it may be granted us through grace; that we may effect that which we cannot fulfil through ourselves; that we may not, ignorant of God's righteousness, and going about to stablish our own, fail to submit ourselves unto the righteousness of God.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Iniquity. Hebrew, "lying. "Let me not imitate the wicked. (Haydock)
Remit the punishment of my sins, (Psalm cvi. 17.; Calmet)
also original sin, and its effects. (St. Hilary)
Protect me from falling. (Worthington)
